title: T-014 — API: BOM approve and publish workflow (POST .../approve, POST .../publish)
prompt:
# T-014 — API: BOM approve and publish workflow (POST .../approve, POST .../publish)

PRD: docs/prd/03-TECHNICAL-PRD.md §7.4, §7.6
Project root: /Users/mariuszkrawczyk/Projects/monopilot-kira

## Goal
BOM approval + publish endpoints; supersede prior active version atomically.

Rollback by publishing a prior version simply means re-running publish on that version. Both endpoints write audit_log rows action='bom.approve' / 'bom.publish'.

## Implementation contract
See `## Goal` + scope_files + acceptance_criteria below; preserve prototype parity, server-action semantics, RLS/org-scoping, and module red-lines listed in `## Risk red lines`.

## Files
- app/api/technical/items/[item_code]/boms/[version]/approve/route.ts [create]
- app/api/technical/items/[item_code]/boms/[version]/publish/route.ts [create]
- lib/technical/bom/workflow.ts [create]
- tests/api/bom-workflow.test.ts [create]

## Out of scope
- Version diff
- Generator

## Acceptance criteria
1. Given draft BOM, when /publish runs, then 422 because V-TEC-10 unmet.
2. Given approved v2 with active v1, when /publish on v2, then v1.status='superseded', v2.status='active' atomically.
3. Given user without technical.bom.approve, when /approve runs, then 403.
4. Given approve runs, when audit_log queried, then a row with action='bom.approve' exists.

## Test strategy
- RED tests for V-TEC-10 + supersede atomicity.
- Run: pnpm vitest run tests/api/bom-workflow.test.ts

## Risk red lines
- Do not allow approving a BOM with cycles (revalidate cycle-check at approve time).
- FG is canonical; FA is legacy alias only — do not introduce FA-* identifiers in new schemas, code, labels, tests.
- D365 is optional integration; never use D365 IDs as hard FKs — d365_item_id is TEXT soft reference only.
- Released BOM/factory_spec edits clone-on-write a new version; never mutate an approved/released row in place.
- Shared BOM SSOT is canonical — Technical, NPD, Planning, Production, Warehouse and Finance read the same bom_headers/lines/co_products tables.
